expectedAssertions: report-quality preserves the review-scope mapping actionability question in JSON and Markdown
report-quality emits a review-existing-fixture action for the promoted public fixture
Markdown renders External Evidence Review Scope Mapping with broad-review and unmapped-output rejects
shareable output contains no local absolute paths or private app identifiers
privateDataPolicy: This materialized starter is synthetic. Use source reports only to choose report shape; do not copy private app code, local paths, screenshots, app-specific identifiers, or proprietary text.

promotion.promotionPolicy: Promotion is explicit maintainer work. ShipGuard writes starter files only to the requested output directory and does not auto-copy them into the repository.
reviewChecklist: Confirm the fixture is synthetic and public-safe.
Confirm no private app code, screenshots, local paths, tokens, app identifiers, or proprietary text were copied.
Confirm scopeBoundary.shipguardOnly and targetAppsReadOnly remain explicit.
Confirm scoring the promoted fixture does not emit recursive fixtureCandidates.
Register the fixture in validation only after the report-quality check passes.

publicFixtureRecipe: Create a minimal synthetic ShipGuard report pair that proves final security-review evidence maps reviewed surfaces to the stable-v4 release scope instead of relying on broad review wording.
sourceQuestion: Does stable-publication require final security-review evidence to map reviewed surfaces to the stable-v4 release scope instead of using broad review wording?
